component.Name: CLI
component.Responsibility: Expose all system operations to the user via a command-line interface. Route user commands to the appropriate components and present results.

requirements.Title: Validate command
requirements.Statement: WHEN the user runs the validate command, the CLI shall invoke the Parser and Validator and display the results to the user.

requirements.Title: Model validation
requirements.Statement: WHEN the user requests validation, the system shall verify that the system model is internally consistent and all cross-references resolve.

requirements.Title: Assemble command
requirements.Statement: WHEN the user runs the assemble command, the CLI shall invoke the Parser, Validator, and Assembler and write the generated context to disk.

requirements.Title: Agent context generation
requirements.Statement: WHEN the user requests context assembly, the system shall produce a resolved context for each affected component, suitable for consumption by an AI coding agent.

requirements.Title: Sync command
requirements.Statement: WHEN the user runs the sync command, the CLI shall invoke the ChangeDetector and Assembler and regenerate only the affected components.

requirements.Title: Incremental sync
requirements.Statement: WHEN the user requests a sync, the system shall regenerate only the components affected by model changes, not the entire codebase.

requirements.Title: Trace command
requirements.Statement: WHEN the user runs the trace command with an element id, the CLI shall invoke the Tracer and display the traceability chain for that element.

requirements.Title: Traceability
requirements.Statement: The system shall maintain traceability from requirements to components to test specifications.

requirements.Title: Verify command
requirements.Statement: WHEN the user runs the verify command, the CLI shall invoke the CoverageAnalyser and display the coverage report.

requirements.Title: Coverage analysis
requirements.Statement: WHEN the user requests verification, the system shall identify any requirements not covered by at least one test specification.

requirements.Title: Model directory option
requirements.Statement: The CLI shall accept a path to the system model directory, defaulting to sys/ in the current working directory.

requirements.Title: System model definition
requirements.Statement: The system shall accept a human-readable, text-based system model as the single source of truth for a software system.

requirements.Title: Serve command
requirements.Statement: WHEN the user runs the serve command, the CLI shall invoke the Visualiser and launch a local HTTP server that renders the system model as an interactive canvas in a browser.

requirements.Title: Visual model representation
requirements.Statement: WHEN the user requests a visual view of the system model, the system shall display the components, their dependencies, interfaces, and traceability links in a graphical format.

test_specs.Title: Validate a clean model prints valid and exits zero
test_specs.Given: a model directory whose parsed SystemModel has no validation errors
test_specs.Expect: stdout includes the word "valid", exit code is 0

test_specs.Title: Validate a broken model prints errors and exits non-zero
test_specs.Given: a model directory whose parsed SystemModel produces at least one validation error
test_specs.Expect: stdout or stderr includes the error message with file path and line number, exit code is non-zero

test_specs.Title: Parse error prints file path and exits non-zero
test_specs.Given: a model directory where one .req file has a syntax error
test_specs.Expect: output contains the offending file name and exit code is non-zero

test_specs.Title: Assemble writes one JSON file per component
test_specs.Given: a valid model directory with three components and an empty output directory
test_specs.Expect: three <component-id>.json files exist under the output directory, exit code is 0

test_specs.Title: Assemble refuses when model is invalid
test_specs.Given: a model directory whose parsed SystemModel has a validation error
test_specs.Expect: no files are written, exit code is non-zero, stderr mentions the validation error

test_specs.Title: Serve validates port argument
test_specs.Given: an invocation "blueprint serve --port not-a-number"
test_specs.Expect: exit code is non-zero and stderr mentions "port"

test_specs.Title: Serve rejects missing port value
test_specs.Given: an invocation "blueprint serve --port" with no value
test_specs.Expect: exit code is non-zero

test_specs.Title: Sync with no baseline writes everything and saves baseline
test_specs.Given: a valid model directory and no existing baseline file
test_specs.When: the user runs "blueprint sync <sys-dir> <out-dir>" from a working directory with no .blueprint/baseline.json
test_specs.Expect: AgentContext files are written for every component, a .blueprint/baseline.json is created, and exit code is 0

test_specs.Title: Sync with unchanged model writes nothing new
test_specs.Given: a baseline that matches the current model
test_specs.Expect: stdout reports no changes, no files are regenerated, exit code is 0

test_specs.Title: Sync regenerates only affected components
test_specs.Given: a baseline and a current model where only one component has changed
test_specs.Expect: only the affected component's AgentContext is written and the baseline is updated, exit code is 0

test_specs.Title: Forward trace prints satisfied_by and verified_by
test_specs.Given: a model directory where REQ-001 is satisfied by COMP-A and verified by TC-001
test_specs.Expect: stdout contains "COMP-A" and "TC-001", exit code is 0

test_specs.Title: Backward trace from test spec lists requirements
test_specs.Given: a model directory where TC-001 verifies REQ-001
test_specs.When: the user runs "blueprint trace TC-001 backward <dir>"
test_specs.Expect: stdout contains "REQ-001", exit code is 0

test_specs.Title: Trace of unknown id exits non-zero
test_specs.Given: a model directory with no element id "REQ-NONE"
test_specs.Expect: exit code is non-zero

test_specs.Title: Verify prints coverage percentage
test_specs.Given: a model directory with at least one verified requirement
test_specs.Expect: stdout contains a percentage and the coverage summary line

test_specs.Title: Verify lists uncovered requirements
test_specs.Given: a model directory with one requirement and no test specs
test_specs.Expect: stdout lists the uncovered requirement id, exit code is non-zero

test_specs.Title: Verify returns zero when fully covered and allocated
test_specs.Given: a model where every requirement is verified and every system requirement is satisfied by a component
test_specs.Expect: exit code is 0

test_specs.Title: Positional directory argument is used
test_specs.Given: a valid model directory at path "./custom/sys"
test_specs.Expect: the CLI parses that directory (not the default sys/) and exits 0

test_specs.Title: Default directory is sys when no argument given
test_specs.Given: a working directory containing a valid sys/ folder
test_specs.When: the user runs "blueprint validate" with no path
test_specs.Expect: the CLI reads from "sys" relative to the working directory and exits 0

test_specs.Title: Unknown subcommand prints usage and exits non-zero
test_specs.Given: an invocation "blueprint bogus"
test_specs.Expect: stderr contains "usage" or "unknown command", exit code is non-zero

test_specs.Title: Parse valid system model
test_specs.Given: a sys/ directory containing valid .req, .iface, .component, and .testspec files
test_specs.Expect: a structured SystemModel with all elements populated and no errors

test_specs.Title: Reject non-text model files
test_specs.Given: a sys/ directory containing a binary file with a .req extension
test_specs.Expect: a syntax error reported with file path and description

test_specs.Title: Handle empty model directory
test_specs.Given: a sys/ directory with no model files
test_specs.Expect: a valid but empty SystemModel with no errors

test_specs.Title: Valid model passes validation
test_specs.Given: a SystemModel where all cross-references resolve
test_specs.Expect: ValidationResult{valid: true, errors: []}

test_specs.Title: Broken satisfies reference detected
test_specs.Given: a component with satisfies: REQ-NONEXISTENT
test_specs.Expect: ValidationResult{valid: false} with an error referencing the broken link

test_specs.Title: Broken verifies reference detected
test_specs.Given: a test spec with verifies: REQ-NONEXISTENT
test_specs.Expect: ValidationResult{valid: false} with an error referencing the broken link

test_specs.Title: Broken depends_on reference detected
test_specs.Given: a component with depends_on: COMP-NONEXISTENT
test_specs.Expect: ValidationResult{valid: false} with an error referencing the broken link

test_specs.Title: Assemble context for a single component
test_specs.Given: a valid SystemModel with one component COMP-001 that satisfies REQ-001, consumes InterfaceA, and is verified by TC-001
test_specs.When: context assembly is requested for COMP-001
test_specs.Expect: AgentContext containing the component definition, REQ-001, InterfaceA, and TC-001

test_specs.Title: Context includes parent system requirements
test_specs.Given: a component with derived requirement BP-001-P01 (derived_from: BP-001)
test_specs.When: context is assembled for that component
test_specs.Expect: AgentContext includes both BP-001-P01 and BP-001

test_specs.Title: Context resolves interface references
test_specs.Given: a component that consumes InterfaceA, which references InterfaceB in a field type
test_specs.When: context is assembled for that component
test_specs.Expect: AgentContext includes both InterfaceA and InterfaceB

test_specs.Title: Forward trace from requirement
test_specs.Given: REQ-001 satisfied by COMP-001, verified by TC-001
test_specs.When: forward trace is requested for REQ-001
test_specs.Expect: TraceResult showing COMP-001 in satisfied_by and TC-001 in verified_by

test_specs.Title: Backward trace from test spec
test_specs.Given: TC-001 verifies REQ-001
test_specs.When: backward trace is requested for TC-001
test_specs.Expect: TraceResult showing REQ-001 as the traced requirement

test_specs.Title: Trace follows derived_from chain
test_specs.Given: BP-001-P01 derived_from BP-001
test_specs.When: backward trace is requested for BP-001-P01
test_specs.Expect: TraceResult includes BP-001 as the root system requirement

test_specs.Title: Full traceability matrix
test_specs.Given: a valid SystemModel with requirements, components, and test specs
test_specs.Expect: every requirement appears with its satisfying components and verifying test specs

test_specs.Title: Detect uncovered requirement
test_specs.Given: REQ-001 has no test spec with verifies: REQ-001
test_specs.Expect: CoverageReport with REQ-001 in uncovered_requirements

test_specs.Title: Detect unallocated requirement
test_specs.Given: REQ-001 has no component with satisfies: REQ-001
test_specs.Expect: CoverageReport with REQ-001 in uncovered_requirements

test_specs.Title: Full coverage reports 100%
test_specs.Given: every requirement is verified by at least one test spec
test_specs.Expect: CoverageReport{coverage_percentage: 100.0, uncovered_requirements: []}

test_specs.Title: Coverage metrics are accurate
test_specs.Given: 4 requirements, 3 covered by test specs, 1 uncovered
test_specs.Expect: CoverageReport{total_requirements: 4, covered_requirements: 3, coverage_percentage: 75.0}

test_specs.Title: Sync regenerates only affected components
test_specs.Given: COMP-001 is in the ChangeSet, COMP-003 is not
test_specs.Expect: context is assembled for COMP-001 only, COMP-003 is untouched

test_specs.Title: Sync with no changes is a no-op
test_specs.Given: no model files changed since the last sync
test_specs.Expect: ChangeSet{affected_components: [], changes: []} and no context is assembled

test_specs.Title: Visual output includes all components
test_specs.Given: a SystemModel with 3 components
test_specs.Expect: output contains all 3 components with their names

test_specs.Title: Visual output shows dependencies
test_specs.Given: COMP-002 depends_on COMP-001
test_specs.Expect: output shows a dependency link from COMP-002 to COMP-001

test_specs.Title: Visual output shows traceability
test_specs.Given: COMP-001 satisfies REQ-001, TC-001 verifies REQ-001
test_specs.Expect: output shows satisfy and verify links
